zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabelle de Vitre於1100年出生在法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉維特雷時，她的父親André de Vitré Seigneur de Vitré35歲，母親Agnes Mortain47歲。她和Hugues de Craon, I Seigneur de Craon至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1162年享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Vitre
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Argentina): adaptation of a surname such as Greek   Mitropoulos   and Italian   Mitri  .
Albanian and Romanian: from the personal name Mitre, a short form of Albanian Dhimitër, Romanian Dumitru, from classical Greek Dēmētrios (see Dimitris ).

variants.name: Mitropoulos
meanings: Greek: patronymic from the personal name Mitros + the ending -poulos (from Latin pullus ‘nestling, chick’), which occurs chiefly in the Peloponnese. Compare Metropoulos .

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabelle de Vitre was born in 1100, in Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Brittany, France, her father, André de Vitré Seigneur de Vitré, was 35 and her mother, Agnes Mortain, was 47. She had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Hugues de Craon, I Seigneur de Craon. She died in 1162, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabelle de Vitre est née en 1100, à Vitré, Ille-et-Vilaine, Bretagne, France, son père, André de Vitré Seigneur de Vitré, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Agnes Mortain, avait 47 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Hugues de Craon, I Seigneur de Craon. Elle est décédée en 1162, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle de Vitre(이)가 1100년 프랑스 브레타그니 이레-트-빌레이인 비트e에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 André de Vitré Seigneur de Vitré(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Mortain(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 Hugues de Craon, I Seigneur de Craon(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1162년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.
